title: KissBridge::with_epoch uses debug_assert for security-critical epoch validation
description:
File: rust/lichen-kiss/src/bridge.rs
Lines: 157-166

The `with_epoch` function uses `debug_assert!` to validate that epoch >= 128 per spec section 4.4. This assertion is stripped in release builds, allowing callers to pass invalid epochs (0-127) that violate the protocol's replay protection requirements.

```rust
pub fn with_epoch(epoch: u8) -> Self {
    debug_assert!(
        epoch >= 128,
        "SECURITY: epoch MUST be in [128, 255] per spec section 4.4"
    );
```

Impact: In release builds, an invalid epoch value could weaken replay protection. The spec requires epochs to start in [128, 255] so that half-space replay arithmetic treats new frames as ahead of stale receiver windows.

Fix: Replace `debug_assert!` with either:
1. A regular `assert!` (panic on invalid input), or
2. Return a `Result<Self, Error>` and validate properly
close_reason: Fixed: Changed debug_assert! to assert! in with_epoch() and added validation to set_epoch() - both now panic on invalid epochs (<128) in all builds per spec section 4.4. Added 6 security tests.
